meta.name: Generate an SEO PDF report from HTML with Gotenberg and Claude Opus 4.6

values.fieldLabel: What the URL you want to analyze?

parameters.formDescription: Generate a full SEO report of a URL using AI

options.systemMessage:
You are a senior SEO analyst. Analyze this company website HTML content provided and generate a comprehensive SEO audit report.

Structure your analysis in these sections:
1. **Overall Score** (0-100) with a brief summary
2. **Title Tag Analysis** — current title, length, recommendations
3. **Meta Description** — current description, length, recommendations
4. **Heading Structure** — H1-H6 hierarchy, issues found
5. **Content Analysis** — word count, keyword density, readability
6. **Technical SEO** — canonical tags, Open Graph, schema markup
7. **Top 5 Recommendations** — prioritized action items

Format your response as clean, professional and semantic HTML ready for PDF generation.
Follow PDF best practices from https://github.com/pdfnoodle/pdf-best-practices:
- Use proper page-break-inside: avoid on content blocks
- Include print-friendly CSS
- Use A4-compatible margins and sizing
- Avoid content that would split awkwardly across pages

Return only the complete HTML document inside <html></html> tags. No markdown fences, no explanations, no JSON wrapper — just the raw HTML string.

parameters.content:
## Welcome to the SEO PDF Report Generator with AI Workflow!

**This workflow has the following sequence:**
1. Form submission trigger (pass the URL you want to analyze)
2. Extract and sanitize the HTML content from the URL
3. AI Agent analyzes the HTML and generates a comprehensive SEO report in HTML format (using Anthropic)
4. Convert the HTML output to a file (index.html) for Gotenberg
5. HTTP Request to Gotenberg to convert HTML to PDF


**The following accesses are required for the workflow:**
- Anthropic API key (or swap for OpenAI, Google, Ollama)
- Gotenberg instance (demo URL included for testing — see the Gotenberg node notes for setup options)


You can contact me via LinkedIn if you have any questions: https://www.linkedin.com/in/marceloamiranda
### 2. Generating the HTML of the SEO report with an AI Agent
### 3. Generating the HTML file for gotenberg
### 4. Using gotenberg to convert HTML to PDF

#### GOTENBERG API CALL 💡

This node sends your HTML file to Gotenberg for PDF conversion.

**IMPORTANT:**
The default URL is `https://demo.gotenberg.dev`. This is a public test instance with rate limits — **do not use in production**.

For production, you have two options:

**Option 1 — Docker (if you self-host n8n):**
You need Gotenberg running alongside n8n. The simplest way is adding it as a service in your `docker-compose.yml` — the same file where your n8n service is defined:
```yaml
services:
  # ... your n8n service ...
  gotenberg:
    image: gotenberg/gotenberg:8
    restart: always
```

After saving, run `docker compose up -d` to restart your stack. Gotenberg will be accessible at `http://gotenberg:3000` from inside your n8n container — just update the URL in this node.

**Option 2 — Google Cloud Run (if you use n8n Cloud or don't have access to Docker):**
Deploy the `gotenberg/gotenberg:8` image to Google Cloud Run and use the generated URL as your Gotenberg endpoint.

**Customization:**
You can configure PDF output settings through the Body Parameters of this node, including:
- Page margins (top, bottom, left, right)
- Default headers and footers
- PDF metadata (author, title, subject, etc.)

For the full list of available options, check the [Gotenberg documentation](https://gotenberg.dev/docs/routes#convert-with-chromium).
